---
title: "STAT 544 Victims"
author: "Ramiro Granados"
date: "2026-02-25"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
```

```{r}
victims_raw <- read_csv("Victims.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  clean_names()  # makes names like case_id, victim_age, etc.

glimpse(victims_raw)

# Convert to numeric 
to_num <- function(x) readr::parse_number(as.character(x))

#deal with blanks n all that
na_if_unknown_chr <- function(x) {
  x <- str_trim(as.character(x))
  x <- na_if(x, "")
  x <- na_if(x, "-")
  x
}
```

```{r}

victims_clean <- victims_raw %>%
  mutate(
  
    across(where(is.character), ~ str_trim(.x)),

    victim_seating_position_num = to_num(victim_seating_position),
    victim_ejected_num          = to_num(victim_ejected),

    victim_seating_position = na_if_unknown_chr(victim_seating_position),
    victim_ejected          = na_if_unknown_chr(victim_ejected),
    victim_safety_equip_1    = na_if_unknown_chr(victim_safety_equip_1),
    victim_safety_equip_2    = na_if_unknown_chr(victim_safety_equip_2),
    victim_sex              = na_if_unknown_chr(victim_sex),
    city                    = na_if_unknown_chr(city),
    county                  = na_if_unknown_chr(county)
  )

victims_clean %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.x)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "col", values_to = "na_count") %>%
  arrange(desc(na_count)) %>%
  print(n = 50)
```

```{r}

#Logic & Integrity Checks

# check unique
victims_clean %>%
  count(case_id, party_number, victim_number, name = "n") %>%
  filter(n > 1) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  print(n = 20)

victims_clean %>%
  summarise(
    age_min = min(victim_age, na.rm = TRUE),
    age_max = max(victim_age, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_age_0 = sum(victim_age == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_age_998 = sum(victim_age == 998, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

victims_clean <- victims_clean %>%
  mutate(
    victim_age_original = victim_age,
    victim_age = na_if(victim_age, 0),
    victim_age = na_if(victim_age, 998),
    victim_age_missing_flag = if_else(is.na(victim_age), 1L, 0L)
  )

victims_clean <- victims_clean %>%
  mutate(
    victim_age = if_else(!is.na(victim_age) & victim_age > 110, 110, victim_age)
  )

victims_clean %>%
  count(accident_year) %>%
  arrange(accident_year)
```

```{r}
# Transformations

victims_math <- victims_clean %>%
  mutate(
    victim_age_z = as.numeric(scale(victim_age))  # if we got NA age then the z score will also be NA
  )
```

```{r}

victims_feat <- victims_math %>%
  mutate(
    age_group = case_when(
      is.na(victim_age)            ~ "Unknown",
      victim_age < 13              ~ "Child",
      victim_age >= 13 & victim_age < 18 ~ "Teen",
      victim_age >= 18 & victim_age < 35 ~ "Young Adult",
      victim_age >= 35 & victim_age < 55 ~ "Adult",
      victim_age >= 55             ~ "Senior"
    ),
    age_group = factor(age_group,
                       levels = c("Child","Teen","Young Adult","Adult","Senior","Unknown"))
  )


city_counts <- victims_feat %>% count(city, sort = TRUE)
rare_cities <- city_counts %>% filter(n < 200) %>% pull(city)

victims_feat <- victims_feat %>%
  mutate(
    city_collapsed = if_else(city %in% rare_cities | is.na(city), "Other/Unknown", city)
  )

victims_feat <- victims_feat %>%
  mutate(
    equip1_known = if_else(is.na(victim_safety_equip_1), 0L, 1L),
    equip2_known = if_else(is.na(victim_safety_equip_2), 0L, 1L),
    any_equip_known = if_else(equip1_known == 1L | equip2_known == 1L, 1L, 0L)
  )
```

```{r}
victims_final <- victims_feat %>%
  mutate(
    victim_sex = factor(victim_sex),
    victim_role = factor(victim_role),
    victim_degree_of_injury = factor(victim_degree_of_injury),
    victim_seating_position_raw = factor(victim_seating_position),
    victim_ejected_raw = factor(victim_ejected)
  )

# Output: cleaned dataset + quick summary tables

glimpse(victims_final)

# Save cleaned data for the rest of your workflow
write_csv(victims_final, "Victims_cleaned.csv")

# A few quick summary views (useful in Rmd)
victims_final %>% count(victim_sex, sort = TRUE)
victims_final %>% count(victim_degree_of_injury, sort = TRUE)
victims_final %>% count(age_group, sort = TRUE)
victims_final %>% count(city_collapsed, sort = TRUE)

```

```{r}
glimpse(victims_final)
```




```{r}
levels(victims_final$victim_sex)
```

```{r}
table(victims_final$victim_sex, useNA = "ifany")
```

```{r}
#We run t-test on F vs M only. the two-sample t-test because the dataset has another cat (‘X’, n=18) and missing values. Including >2 levels violates t-test assumptions.

victims_sex_FM <- victims_final %>%
  filter(victim_sex %in% c("F", "M")) %>%   # drops X and NA
  droplevels() %>%
  filter(!is.na(victim_age))                # ensure age is present

table(victims_sex_FM$victim_sex)

t.test(victim_age ~ victim_sex, data = victims_sex_FM)

mean_age <- victims_sex_FM %>%
  group_by(victim_sex) %>%
  summarise(mean_age = mean(victim_age), sd_age = sd(victim_age), n = n())

mean_age
```
